name: Hazelnut Spirals
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Butter, cold; cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
DESCRIPTION: Definitely a grown-up version of the classic pinwheel cookie, these feature a spiced hazelnut filling.
Combine flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a food processor. Add butter pieces and process until it resembles coarse meal. In a bowl, lightly beat egg and vanilla to combine, then add to food processor, pulsing just until blended. Gather dough into a ball, wrap in plastic, and set aside at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Combine all filling ingredients EXCEPT butter in a small bowl. Roll the dough on a lightly floured surface into an 18- by 6-by 1/8-inch rectangle. Turn so the long side faces you. Gently spread 1 tablespoon softened butter evenly on the rectangle. Sprinkle nut/spice mixture over the dough, covering all but about 1/2 inch along the far, long edge. Use a rolling pin to lightly press nuts into the dough. Beginning with the side closest to you, roll the dough tightly in a jellyroll fashion. Pinch the seam to seal. Wrap tightly in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 3 hours.
Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease 3-4 large baking sheets. Using a sharp knife, cut the roll into 3/8-inch slices. Arrange the slices about 1 inch apart on the prepared baking sheets. Bake for 9-13 minutes or until lightly golden on top and golden brown at the bottom. Remove from oven to wire racks to cool. Store airtight at room temperature for up to 1 week, or freeze up to three months.
